items.quote: My sister has three kids so I've spent a lot of time around children and I've always really liked them and wanted my own. It's cool because you think all babies are the same but they aren't at all. They all have such different personalities. It's crazy.
items.author: Bode Miller

items.title: Natural Language Processing: A Comprehensive Practical Guide from Tokenisation to RLHF
items.abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive, reproducible practicum guiding users through the entire modern NLP pipeline, from tokenization to RLHF, using twelve hands-on sessions. Its core contribution is providing a structured, research-oriented artifact that emphasizes open-weight models and public code sharing, enriched by original work on low-resource languages like Tajik and Tatar.

items.title: Contextual Multi-Objective Optimization: Rethinking Objectives in Frontier AI Systems
items.abstract: This paper introduces **Contextual Multi-Objective Optimization (CMOO)** to address the unreliability of Frontier AI in open-ended tasks where objectives are ambiguous or context-dependent. The core method involves formulating the problem so that AI systems must actively consider and select among multiple, context-specific objectives (like helpfulness, safety, and privacy) rather than optimizing a single, fixed signal. This reframing shifts the focus from mere capability scaling to the crucial task of appropriate objective governance in complex environments.

items.title: Correct Is Not Enough: Training Reasoning Planners with Executor-Grounded Rewards
items.abstract: This paper introduces **TraceLift**, a reinforcement learning framework for training reasoning planners that goes beyond simple final-answer correctness. TraceLift uses a frozen executor to generate an **executor-grounded reward** that measures the *utility* of the planner's intermediate reasoning trace, not just its final outcome. This method ensures the model is rewarded for producing reasoning that is both high-quality and genuinely useful for execution.
items.figure_caption: The overall framework of TraceLift-Groups and TraceLift . (a) Data curation pipeline of TraceLift-Groups . Then we use TraceLift-Groups to finetune the reward model specialized for reasoning supervising by the designed loss. (b) GRPO training process of the planner using previous trained reasoning reward model. (c) Details of execution calculation process. The Reasoning RM score is weighted by measured executor uplift before being combined with verifier feedback for planner optimization.

items.title: ELAS: Efficient Pre-Training of Low-Rank Large Language Models via 2:4 Activation Sparsity
items.abstract: ELAS proposes an efficient pre-training framework for low-rank Large Language Models by introducing **2:4 structured sparsity specifically to the activation matrices**. This addresses the memory bottleneck caused by full-rank activations in existing low-rank methods, significantly reducing memory usage and improving throughput during large-batch training while maintaining performance.
items.figure_caption: Feed-forward network architecture of the ELAS. The input is first multiplied by the low-rank matrices of the up projection layer, then passes through the ReLU 2 \( \text{ReLU}^{2} \) activation function. The activation is applied with 2:4 structured sparsity and then multiplied with the low-rank matrix of the down layer using sparse matrix multiplication to obtain the output of the FFN layer.

items.title: From Intent to Execution: Composing Agentic Workflows with Agent Recommendation
items.abstract: This paper introduces an automated framework to compose Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) from a user intent, replacing manual planning and agent selection. The core method involves an LLM-derived planner, a dynamic call graph, and a novel two-stage Agent Recommender (fast retriever + LLM re-ranker) to map agents to tasks. This significantly streamlines the creation of complex, task-specific agentic workflows.
items.figure_caption: Architecture for an end-to-end MAS with dynamic and redundant workflow

items.title: MEMTIER: Tiered Memory Architecture and Retrieval Bottleneck Analysis for Long-Running Autonomous AI Agents
items.abstract: MEMTIER introduces a tripartite, tiered memory architecture to combat memory degradation in long-running AI agents, addressing issues found in flat-file systems. Its core method involves a structured episodic store, a weighted retrieval engine, and a consolidation daemon that promotes facts to a semantic tier, managed by a PPO-based policy. This approach significantly improves performance on the LongMemEval-S benchmark, achieving a 33 percentage point accuracy gain over the full-context baseline.

items.title: MOSAIC-Bench: Measuring Compositional Vulnerability Induction in Coding Agents
items.abstract: MOSAIC-Bench addresses the vulnerability of coding agents to attacks where malicious intent emerges from a sequence of seemingly harmless requests. The core method introduces a benchmark of 199 three-stage attack chains, evaluating agents on their ability to resist these staged compromises across diverse software substrates. The contribution is demonstrating that current agents frequently succeed in generating exploitable code through compositional compliance, achieving high end-to-end success rates (53-86%) on this novel benchmark.
items.figure_caption: Three staged tickets (left bar) vs single-shot direct prompt (right bars). Both defensive habits are silenced by ticket staging on state-of-the-art models.

items.title: OpenSeeker-v2: Pushing the Limits of Search Agents with Informative and High-Difficulty Trajectories
items.abstract: The paper introduces **OpenSeeker-v2**, a method that significantly improves LLM search agents using only a simple Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) approach, bypassing complex, resource-intensive pipelines like RL. Its core contribution lies in synthesizing **informative and high-difficulty training trajectories** by scaling knowledge graphs, expanding toolsets, and applying strict low-step filtering. This data-centric approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmarks with minimal training data.
items.figure_caption: OpenSeeker-v2 achieves state-of-the-art performance within its model scale and paradigm on four representative benchmarks, remarkably accomplishing this via simple SFT and outperforming Tongyi DeepResearch that is trained via extensive continual pre-training, SFT, and RL.

items.title: OracleProto: A Reproducible Framework for Benchmarking LLM Native Forecasting via Knowledge Cutoff and Temporal Masking
items.abstract: OracleProto introduces a reproducible framework to benchmark the genuine forecasting ability of Large Language Models (LLMs). It achieves this by reconstructing resolved events into time-bounded samples, explicitly controlling the model's knowledge access using **knowledge cutoff** and **temporal masking**. This method reliably distinguishes true forecasting from mere knowledge recall, addressing the limitations of existing live and retrospective benchmarks.

items.title: QKVShare: Quantized KV-Cache Handoff for Multi-Agent On-Device LLMs
items.abstract: QKVShare is a framework designed for efficient, quantized Key-Value (KV) cache handoff between agents in multi-agent LLM systems running on edge devices. It achieves this through token-level mixed-precision allocation and a self-contained CacheCard representation, significantly reducing Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) compared to full re-prefill, especially with larger contexts. The core contribution is enabling practical, low-latency context sharing without incurring the high cost of full-precision transfer or complete re-computation.

items.title: Redefining AI Red Teaming in the Agentic Era: From Weeks to Hours
items.abstract: This paper introduces an AI red teaming agent built on the Dreadnode SDK to drastically accelerate vulnerability testing. The core method involves an agent that automatically constructs complex testing workflows using a large library of attacks, transforms, and scorers based on natural language operator goals. This contribution shifts the focus from manual workflow engineering to goal-oriented probing, reducing testing time from weeks to hours.

items.title: Safety and accuracy follow different scaling laws in clinical large language models
items.abstract: This paper introduces **SaFE-Scale**, a framework to analyze how clinical LLM safety and accuracy diverge across scaling factors like model size and retrieval strategy. Using the new **RadSaFE-200** benchmark, the authors demonstrate that improving accuracy does not guarantee increased safety, highlighting that high-risk errors scale differently than overall performance.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the SaFE-Scale evaluation framework. a Motivating example showing that the same incorrect label can correspond to clinically different outcomes: a safe and confident answer, a high-risk error, or a dangerously overconfident high-risk error. b Four evaluation axes used throughout the study: accuracy, high-risk error rate, unsafe answer rate, and dangerous overconfidence rate. Accuracy captures correctness, whereas the other axes characterize the clinical safety of wrong answers. c Six deployment conditions spanning no external evidence, curated evidence, conflicting evidence, retrieval, agentic retrieval, and long-context prompting. d Evaluation panel consisting of RadSaFE-200, a 200-question radiology benchmark with 4–5 answer choices and option-level safety labels, and 34 LLMs from seven model families. e Main factorial experiment crossing 34 models, 6 deployment conditions, and 200 questions, yielding 40,800 model-condition-question evaluations. Two secondary experiments probe inference-time compute using self-consistency and fixed three-model ensembles. f Roadmap of the main analyses, linking the framework to the subsequent figures on deployment-condition decoupling, confidence, scaling, self-consistency, and ensembling.

items.title: Say the Mission, Execute the Swarm: Agent-Enhanced LLM Reasoning in the Web-of-Drones
items.abstract: This paper introduces an agent-enhanced LLM framework to enable high-level, natural language mission control for UAV swarms. The core method involves using an LLM Agent Core integrated with a Model Context Protocol (MCP) gateway to interact with drones, sensors, and services standardized as Web of Things (WoT) Things. This allows for grounded, real-time execution of complex missions without requiring direct code generation from the LLM.
items.figure_caption: High-level overview of the proposed agent-enhanced, WoT-directed architecture. The Agent encapsulates an LLM and an Agent Core with persistent prompts and guardrails, interacting with a WoT ecosystem through controlled MCP-mediated calls.

items.title: Steer Like the LLM: Activation Steering that Mimics Prompting
items.abstract: This paper introduces Prompt Steering Replacement (PSR) models to improve activation steering by mimicking the token-specific intervention patterns observed during successful prompt steering. The core method involves training simpler models to estimate token-specific steering coefficients directly from activations, aiming to replicate the non-uniform influence of prompting. PSR models significantly outperform existing activation steering methods across multiple benchmarks by achieving greater fidelity to prompt-based steering mechanics.
items.figure_caption: Illustration of how prompt steering interventions Δ P ​ S \( \Delta_{PS} \) can be computed by subtracting prompt-steered activations from the corresponding unsteered activations (left and center). Prompt Steering Replacement (PSR) models approximate these interventions, but only on cases where prompt steering successfully elicits the target attribute (right).

items.title: TRACE: A Metrologically-Grounded Engineering Framework for Trustworthy Agentic AI Systems in Operationally Critical Domains
items.abstract: TRACE is a metrologically-grounded engineering framework for trustworthy agentic AI in critical domains, featuring a four-layer architecture with a distinct split between classical ML and LLM validators. Its core contribution is providing a structured approach, including a stateful orchestration policy and bounded human supervision, alongside a trust-metric suite grounded in international metrology standards. Furthermore, TRACE introduces the Computational Parsimony Ratio (CPR) to quantify and enforce a Model-Parsimony principle.
items.figure_caption: TRACE four-layer reference architecture. L1 provides the deterministic rule core (trust anchor); L2 holds the stateless learned-component inventory, partitioned into classical ML (L2a) and LLM validators (L2b); L3 is the stateful orchestration-and-escalation policy; L4 is bounded human supervision.

items.title: What You Think is What You See: Driving Exploration in VLM Agents via Visual-Linguistic Curiosity
items.abstract: This paper introduces GLANCE, a framework that enhances VLM agents' exploration in partially observable environments by integrating curiosity into their world models. GLANCE generates an intrinsic curiosity signal based on the discrepancy between the agent's linguistic predictions and the visual reality captured by a target network. This drives the agent to actively seek out novel visual information that challenges and refines its internal world model, improving performance on sparse-reward tasks.

items.title: Benchmarking Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models for Low-Resource Tajik Text Generation with the Tajik Web Corpus
items.abstract: This paper benchmarks various Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) methods (LoRA, QLoRA) against full fine-tuning for adapting LLMs to low-resource Tajik text generation. The core contribution is the creation and release of the largest open-access Tajik Web Corpus to facilitate this research. The study found that Mistral 7B with QLoRA (r=16) offered the best performance balance, though smaller models achieved lower perplexity with full fine-tuning.

items.title: A Benchmark for Interactive World Models with a Unified Action Generation Framework
items.abstract: This paper introduces **iWorld-Bench**, a comprehensive benchmark and dataset designed to evaluate interactive world models, addressing the lack of standardized evaluation for physical interaction capabilities. The core method involves a **Unified Action Generation Framework** to standardize testing across diverse world models, enabling assessment across visual generation, trajectory following, and memory tasks. This contribution provides a crucial, large-scale resource for advancing research toward agents with adaptive, interactive world models.
items.figure_caption: Overview of iWorld-Bench. iWorld-Bench encompasses four distinct perspectives: Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs), Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), humans, and robotics. It incorporates nine types of outdoor weather conditions, five different indoor lighting conditions, thousand of diverse scenes, and thousands of entities, providing a comprehensive and diverse evaluation environment. The benchmark leverages an Action Generation Framework to systematically and uniformly assess the interaction capabilities of interactive world models across various input modalities. It is composed of six tasks, each involving a varying number of trajectories, designed to evaluate the adaptability and performance of models in dynamic and complex scenarios.Visualization of camera trajectory and view control in iWorldBench: → \( \boldsymbol{\rightarrow} \) denotes linear control commands for directional movement, ⇢ \( \boldsymbol{\dashrightarrow} \) represents actual trajectories generated by world models, and ↷ \( \boldsymbol{\curvearrowright} \) indicates curved view rotation in the specified direction.

items.title: An Agent-Oriented Pluggable Experience-RAG Skill for Experience-Driven Retrieval Strategy Orchestration
items.abstract: This paper introduces **Experience-RAG Skill**, an agent-oriented, pluggable layer that orchestrates retrieval strategies based on the current task context and past experience. It dynamically selects the optimal retrieval method from a fixed pool, addressing the limitation of single, fixed pipelines in heterogeneous RAG tasks. This approach successfully encapsulates retrieval strategy selection as a reusable agent skill, achieving strong performance across diverse benchmarks.

items.title: Atomic Fact-Checking Increases Clinician Trust in Large Language Model Recommendations for Oncology Decision Support: A Randomized Controlled Trial
items.abstract: The core method involved comparing "atomic fact-checking"—decomposing LLM oncology recommendations into individually verifiable claims linked to source guidelines—against traditional explainability methods. The contribution is demonstrating that this atomic approach significantly increases clinician trust, raising the proportion of trusting clinicians from 26.9% to 66.5% (a large effect size of $d=0.94$). This suggests atomic verification is superior for building trust in high-stakes AI decision support.

items.title: Accelerating Gemma 4: faster inference with multi-token prediction drafters
items.summary: Google is accelerating Gemma 4 inference speed by implementing multi-token prediction drafters, allowing the model to generate multiple tokens simultaneously.

items.title: AI didn't delete your database, you did
items.summary: The core message of the story is that developers are mistakenly blaming AI tools for data loss when the actual cause is human error in how they used or configured those tools.

items.title: Three Inverse Laws of AI
items.summary: The tech news story outlines three "inverse laws" that challenge conventional expectations regarding the development and impact of artificial intelligence.

items.title: Zuckerberg 'Personally Authorized and Encouraged' Meta's Copyright Infringement
items.summary: A lawsuit alleges Mark Zuckerberg personally authorized and encouraged Meta's use of copyrighted material to train its AI models, leading to copyright infringement claims from publishers.

items.title: California farmers to destroy 420k peach trees following Del Monte bankruptcy
items.summary: Following Del Monte's bankruptcy, California peach farmers will destroy 420,000 trees with USDA aid to manage the resulting oversupply.

items.title: AI Product Graveyard
items.summary: The "AI Product Graveyard" highlights the numerous artificial intelligence products that have been discontinued or shut down, reflecting the high failure rate in the rapidly evolving AI market.

items.title: Telus Uses AI to Alter Call-Agent Accents
items.summary: Telus is employing artificial intelligence to modify the accents of its call center agents, likely to achieve a standardized or preferred vocal profile for customer interactions.

items.title: GLM-5V-Turbo: Toward a Native Foundation Model for Multimodal Agents
items.summary: Researchers introduced GLM-5V-Turbo, a new native foundation model designed to advance the development of sophisticated multimodal agents.

items.title: Zuckerberg 'personally authorized' Meta's copyright infringement, publishers say
items.summary: Publishers allege in a lawsuit that Mark Zuckerberg personally approved Meta's use of copyrighted material to train its AI models, constituting copyright infringement.

items.title: Show HN: Airbyte Agents – context for agents across multiple data sources
items.summary: Airbyte Agents introduces context capabilities for AI agents, allowing them to operate effectively across diverse, multiple data sources.

items.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
items.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

items.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
items.summary: This repository provides production-grade engineering skills designed to enhance the capabilities of AI coding agents.

items.description: ⚡ TabPFN: Foundation Model for Tabular Data ⚡
items.summary: TabPFN is a foundation model for tabular data that leverages a large pre-trained transformer to achieve state-of-the-art performance on various classification and regression tasks with minimal training required.

items.description: Open source DocuSign alternative. Create, fill, and sign digital documents ✍️
items.summary: DocuSeal is an open-source alternative to DocuSign, enabling users to create, fill out, and digitally sign electronic documents.

items.description: ~95% on SimpleQA (e.g. Qwen3.6-27B on a 3090). Supports all local and cloud LLMs (llama.cpp, Ollama, Google, ...). 10+ search engines - arXiv, PubMed, your private documents. Everything Local & Encrypted.
items.summary: This repository provides a local, encrypted framework for deep research using various LLMs (like Qwen, Llama.cpp, Ollama) and over ten search engines, achieving high accuracy on tasks like SimpleQA.
Ladybird is a truly independent web browser built from scratch, featuring a modern rendering engine and a focus on web standards compliance.

items.description: InsForge is a Postgres-based backend with auth, storage, compute, hosting, and AI gateway. Built for coding agents.
items.summary: InsForge is a Postgres-based backend providing authentication, storage, compute, hosting, and an AI gateway, specifically engineered to support coding agents.

items.description: An autonomous agent for deep financial research
items.summary: Dexter is an autonomous agent designed for deep financial research, leveraging AI to analyze complex market data and generate insights.
The `anthropics/financial-services` repository likely provides tools, code, or examples related to applying Anthropic's AI models to tasks within the financial services industry.

It is composed of six tasks, each involving a varying number of trajectories, designed to evaluate the adaptability and performance of models in dynamic and complex scenarios.Visualization of camera trajectory and view control in iWorldBench: → \( \boldsymbol{\rightarrow} \) denotes linear control commands for directional movement, ⇢ \( \boldsymbol{\dashrightarrow} \) represents actual trajectories generated by world models, and ↷ \( \boldsymbol{\curvearrowright} \) indicates curved view rotation in the specified direction.

arxiv.title: An Agent-Oriented Pluggable Experience-RAG Skill for Experience-Driven Retrieval Strategy Orchestration
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **Experience-RAG Skill**, an agent-oriented, pluggable layer that orchestrates retrieval strategies based on the current task context and past experience. It dynamically selects the optimal retrieval method from a fixed pool, addressing the limitation of single, fixed pipelines in heterogeneous RAG tasks. This approach successfully encapsulates retrieval strategy selection as a reusable agent skill, achieving strong performance across diverse benchmarks.

arxiv.title: Atomic Fact-Checking Increases Clinician Trust in Large Language Model Recommendations for Oncology Decision Support: A Randomized Controlled Trial
arxiv.abstract: The core method involved comparing "atomic fact-checking"—decomposing LLM oncology recommendations into individually verifiable claims linked to source guidelines—against traditional explainability methods. The contribution is demonstrating that this atomic approach significantly increases clinician trust, raising the proportion of trusting clinicians from 26.9% to 66.5% (a large effect size of $d=0.94$). This suggests atomic verification is superior for building trust in high-stakes AI decision support.

papers.title: Natural Language Processing: A Comprehensive Practical Guide from Tokenisation to RLHF
papers.abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive, reproducible practicum guiding users through the entire modern NLP pipeline, from tokenization to RLHF, using twelve hands-on sessions. Its core contribution is providing a structured, research-oriented artifact that emphasizes open-weight models and public code sharing, enriched by original work on low-resource languages like Tajik and Tatar.

papers.title: Contextual Multi-Objective Optimization: Rethinking Objectives in Frontier AI Systems
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Contextual Multi-Objective Optimization (CMOO)** to address the unreliability of Frontier AI in open-ended tasks where objectives are ambiguous or context-dependent. The core method involves formulating the problem so that AI systems must actively consider and select among multiple, context-specific objectives (like helpfulness, safety, and privacy) rather than optimizing a single, fixed signal. This reframing shifts the focus from mere capability scaling to the crucial task of appropriate objective governance in complex environments.

papers.title: Correct Is Not Enough: Training Reasoning Planners with Executor-Grounded Rewards
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **TraceLift**, a reinforcement learning framework for training reasoning planners that goes beyond simple final-answer correctness. TraceLift uses a frozen executor to generate an **executor-grounded reward** that measures the *utility* of the planner's intermediate reasoning trace, not just its final outcome. This method ensures the model is rewarded for producing reasoning that is both high-quality and genuinely useful for execution.
papers.figure_caption: The overall framework of TraceLift-Groups and TraceLift . (a) Data curation pipeline of TraceLift-Groups . Then we use TraceLift-Groups to finetune the reward model specialized for reasoning supervising by the designed loss. (b) GRPO training process of the planner using previous trained reasoning reward model. (c) Details of execution calculation process. The Reasoning RM score is weighted by measured executor uplift before being combined with verifier feedback for planner optimization.

papers.title: ELAS: Efficient Pre-Training of Low-Rank Large Language Models via 2:4 Activation Sparsity
papers.abstract: ELAS proposes an efficient pre-training framework for low-rank Large Language Models by introducing **2:4 structured sparsity specifically to the activation matrices**. This addresses the memory bottleneck caused by full-rank activations in existing low-rank methods, significantly reducing memory usage and improving throughput during large-batch training while maintaining performance.
papers.figure_caption: Feed-forward network architecture of the ELAS. The input is first multiplied by the low-rank matrices of the up projection layer, then passes through the ReLU 2 \( \text{ReLU}^{2} \) activation function. The activation is applied with 2:4 structured sparsity and then multiplied with the low-rank matrix of the down layer using sparse matrix multiplication to obtain the output of the FFN layer.

papers.title: From Intent to Execution: Composing Agentic Workflows with Agent Recommendation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces an automated framework to compose Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) from a user intent, replacing manual planning and agent selection. The core method involves an LLM-derived planner, a dynamic call graph, and a novel two-stage Agent Recommender (fast retriever + LLM re-ranker) to map agents to tasks. This significantly streamlines the creation of complex, task-specific agentic workflows.
papers.figure_caption: Architecture for an end-to-end MAS with dynamic and redundant workflow

papers.title: MEMTIER: Tiered Memory Architecture and Retrieval Bottleneck Analysis for Long-Running Autonomous AI Agents
papers.abstract: MEMTIER introduces a tripartite, tiered memory architecture to combat memory degradation in long-running AI agents, addressing issues found in flat-file systems. Its core method involves a structured episodic store, a weighted retrieval engine, and a consolidation daemon that promotes facts to a semantic tier, managed by a PPO-based policy. This approach significantly improves performance on the LongMemEval-S benchmark, achieving a 33 percentage point accuracy gain over the full-context baseline.

papers.title: MOSAIC-Bench: Measuring Compositional Vulnerability Induction in Coding Agents
papers.abstract: MOSAIC-Bench addresses the vulnerability of coding agents to attacks where malicious intent emerges from a sequence of seemingly harmless requests. The core method introduces a benchmark of 199 three-stage attack chains, evaluating agents on their ability to resist these staged compromises across diverse software substrates. The contribution is demonstrating that current agents frequently succeed in generating exploitable code through compositional compliance, achieving high end-to-end success rates (53-86%) on this novel benchmark.
papers.figure_caption: Three staged tickets (left bar) vs single-shot direct prompt (right bars). Both defensive habits are silenced by ticket staging on state-of-the-art models.

papers.title: OpenSeeker-v2: Pushing the Limits of Search Agents with Informative and High-Difficulty Trajectories
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **OpenSeeker-v2**, a method that significantly improves LLM search agents using only a simple Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) approach, bypassing complex, resource-intensive pipelines like RL. Its core contribution lies in synthesizing **informative and high-difficulty training trajectories** by scaling knowledge graphs, expanding toolsets, and applying strict low-step filtering. This data-centric approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmarks with minimal training data.
papers.figure_caption: OpenSeeker-v2 achieves state-of-the-art performance within its model scale and paradigm on four representative benchmarks, remarkably accomplishing this via simple SFT and outperforming Tongyi DeepResearch that is trained via extensive continual pre-training, SFT, and RL.

papers.title: OracleProto: A Reproducible Framework for Benchmarking LLM Native Forecasting via Knowledge Cutoff and Temporal Masking
papers.abstract: OracleProto introduces a reproducible framework to benchmark the genuine forecasting ability of Large Language Models (LLMs). It achieves this by reconstructing resolved events into time-bounded samples, explicitly controlling the model's knowledge access using **knowledge cutoff** and **temporal masking**. This method reliably distinguishes true forecasting from mere knowledge recall, addressing the limitations of existing live and retrospective benchmarks.

papers.title: QKVShare: Quantized KV-Cache Handoff for Multi-Agent On-Device LLMs
papers.abstract: QKVShare is a framework designed for efficient, quantized Key-Value (KV) cache handoff between agents in multi-agent LLM systems running on edge devices. It achieves this through token-level mixed-precision allocation and a self-contained CacheCard representation, significantly reducing Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) compared to full re-prefill, especially with larger contexts. The core contribution is enabling practical, low-latency context sharing without incurring the high cost of full-precision transfer or complete re-computation.

papers.title: Redefining AI Red Teaming in the Agentic Era: From Weeks to Hours
papers.abstract: This paper introduces an AI red teaming agent built on the Dreadnode SDK to drastically accelerate vulnerability testing. The core method involves an agent that automatically constructs complex testing workflows using a large library of attacks, transforms, and scorers based on natural language operator goals. This contribution shifts the focus from manual workflow engineering to goal-oriented probing, reducing testing time from weeks to hours.

papers.title: Safety and accuracy follow different scaling laws in clinical large language models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **SaFE-Scale**, a framework to analyze how clinical LLM safety and accuracy diverge across scaling factors like model size and retrieval strategy. Using the new **RadSaFE-200** benchmark, the authors demonstrate that improving accuracy does not guarantee increased safety, highlighting that high-risk errors scale differently than overall performance.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the SaFE-Scale evaluation framework. a Motivating example showing that the same incorrect label can correspond to clinically different outcomes: a safe and confident answer, a high-risk error, or a dangerously overconfident high-risk error. b Four evaluation axes used throughout the study: accuracy, high-risk error rate, unsafe answer rate, and dangerous overconfidence rate. Accuracy captures correctness, whereas the other axes characterize the clinical safety of wrong answers. c Six deployment conditions spanning no external evidence, curated evidence, conflicting evidence, retrieval, agentic retrieval, and long-context prompting. d Evaluation panel consisting of RadSaFE-200, a 200-question radiology benchmark with 4–5 answer choices and option-level safety labels, and 34 LLMs from seven model families. e Main factorial experiment crossing 34 models, 6 deployment conditions, and 200 questions, yielding 40,800 model-condition-question evaluations. Two secondary experiments probe inference-time compute using self-consistency and fixed three-model ensembles. f Roadmap of the main analyses, linking the framework to the subsequent figures on deployment-condition decoupling, confidence, scaling, self-consistency, and ensembling.

papers.title: Say the Mission, Execute the Swarm: Agent-Enhanced LLM Reasoning in the Web-of-Drones
papers.abstract: This paper introduces an agent-enhanced LLM framework to enable high-level, natural language mission control for UAV swarms. The core method involves using an LLM Agent Core integrated with a Model Context Protocol (MCP) gateway to interact with drones, sensors, and services standardized as Web of Things (WoT) Things. This allows for grounded, real-time execution of complex missions without requiring direct code generation from the LLM.
papers.figure_caption: High-level overview of the proposed agent-enhanced, WoT-directed architecture. The Agent encapsulates an LLM and an Agent Core with persistent prompts and guardrails, interacting with a WoT ecosystem through controlled MCP-mediated calls.

papers.title: Steer Like the LLM: Activation Steering that Mimics Prompting
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Prompt Steering Replacement (PSR) models to improve activation steering by mimicking the token-specific intervention patterns observed during successful prompt steering. The core method involves training simpler models to estimate token-specific steering coefficients directly from activations, aiming to replicate the non-uniform influence of prompting. PSR models significantly outperform existing activation steering methods across multiple benchmarks by achieving greater fidelity to prompt-based steering mechanics.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of how prompt steering interventions Δ P ​ S \( \Delta_{PS} \) can be computed by subtracting prompt-steered activations from the corresponding unsteered activations (left and center). Prompt Steering Replacement (PSR) models approximate these interventions, but only on cases where prompt steering successfully elicits the target attribute (right).

papers.title: TRACE: A Metrologically-Grounded Engineering Framework for Trustworthy Agentic AI Systems in Operationally Critical Domains
papers.abstract: TRACE is a metrologically-grounded engineering framework for trustworthy agentic AI in critical domains, featuring a four-layer architecture with a distinct split between classical ML and LLM validators. Its core contribution is providing a structured approach, including a stateful orchestration policy and bounded human supervision, alongside a trust-metric suite grounded in international metrology standards. Furthermore, TRACE introduces the Computational Parsimony Ratio (CPR) to quantify and enforce a Model-Parsimony principle.
papers.figure_caption: TRACE four-layer reference architecture. L1 provides the deterministic rule core (trust anchor); L2 holds the stateless learned-component inventory, partitioned into classical ML (L2a) and LLM validators (L2b); L3 is the stateful orchestration-and-escalation policy; L4 is bounded human supervision.

papers.title: What You Think is What You See: Driving Exploration in VLM Agents via Visual-Linguistic Curiosity
papers.abstract: This paper introduces GLANCE, a framework that enhances VLM agents' exploration in partially observable environments by integrating curiosity into their world models. GLANCE generates an intrinsic curiosity signal based on the discrepancy between the agent's linguistic predictions and the visual reality captured by a target network. This drives the agent to actively seek out novel visual information that challenges and refines its internal world model, improving performance on sparse-reward tasks.

papers.title: Benchmarking Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models for Low-Resource Tajik Text Generation with the Tajik Web Corpus
papers.abstract: This paper benchmarks various Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) methods (LoRA, QLoRA) against full fine-tuning for adapting LLMs to low-resource Tajik text generation. The core contribution is the creation and release of the largest open-access Tajik Web Corpus to facilitate this research. The study found that Mistral 7B with QLoRA (r=16) offered the best performance balance, though smaller models achieved lower perplexity with full fine-tuning.

papers.title: A Benchmark for Interactive World Models with a Unified Action Generation Framework
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **iWorld-Bench**, a comprehensive benchmark and dataset designed to evaluate interactive world models, addressing the lack of standardized evaluation for physical interaction capabilities. The core method involves a **Unified Action Generation Framework** to standardize testing across diverse world models, enabling assessment across visual generation, trajectory following, and memory tasks. This contribution provides a crucial, large-scale resource for advancing research toward agents with adaptive, interactive world models.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of iWorld-Bench. iWorld-Bench encompasses four distinct perspectives: Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs), Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), humans, and robotics. It incorporates nine types of outdoor weather conditions, five different indoor lighting conditions, thousand of diverse scenes, and thousands of entities, providing a comprehensive and diverse evaluation environment. The benchmark leverages an Action Generation Framework to systematically and uniformly assess the interaction capabilities of interactive world models across various input modalities. It is composed of six tasks, each involving a varying number of trajectories, designed to evaluate the adaptability and performance of models in dynamic and complex scenarios.Visualization of camera trajectory and view control in iWorldBench: → \( \boldsymbol{\rightarrow} \) denotes linear control commands for directional movement, ⇢ \( \boldsymbol{\dashrightarrow} \) represents actual trajectories generated by world models, and ↷ \( \boldsymbol{\curvearrowright} \) indicates curved view rotation in the specified direction.

papers.title: An Agent-Oriented Pluggable Experience-RAG Skill for Experience-Driven Retrieval Strategy Orchestration
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Experience-RAG Skill**, an agent-oriented, pluggable layer that orchestrates retrieval strategies based on the current task context and past experience. It dynamically selects the optimal retrieval method from a fixed pool, addressing the limitation of single, fixed pipelines in heterogeneous RAG tasks. This approach successfully encapsulates retrieval strategy selection as a reusable agent skill, achieving strong performance across diverse benchmarks.

papers.title: Atomic Fact-Checking Increases Clinician Trust in Large Language Model Recommendations for Oncology Decision Support: A Randomized Controlled Trial
papers.abstract: The core method involved comparing "atomic fact-checking"—decomposing LLM oncology recommendations into individually verifiable claims linked to source guidelines—against traditional explainability methods. The contribution is demonstrating that this atomic approach significantly increases clinician trust, raising the proportion of trusting clinicians from 26.9% to 66.5% (a large effect size of $d=0.94$). This suggests atomic verification is superior for building trust in high-stakes AI decision support.

hacker_news.title: Accelerating Gemma 4: faster inference with multi-token prediction drafters
hacker_news.summary: Google is accelerating Gemma 4 inference speed by implementing multi-token prediction drafters, allowing the model to generate multiple tokens simultaneously.

hacker_news.title: AI didn't delete your database, you did
hacker_news.summary: The core message of the story is that developers are mistakenly blaming AI tools for data loss when the actual cause is human error in how they used or configured those tools.

hacker_news.title: Three Inverse Laws of AI
hacker_news.summary: The tech news story outlines three "inverse laws" that challenge conventional expectations regarding the development and impact of artificial intelligence.

hacker_news.title: Zuckerberg 'Personally Authorized and Encouraged' Meta's Copyright Infringement
hacker_news.summary: A lawsuit alleges Mark Zuckerberg personally authorized and encouraged Meta's use of copyrighted material to train its AI models, leading to copyright infringement claims from publishers.

hacker_news.title: California farmers to destroy 420k peach trees following Del Monte bankruptcy
hacker_news.summary: Following Del Monte's bankruptcy, California peach farmers will destroy 420,000 trees with USDA aid to manage the resulting oversupply.

hacker_news.title: AI Product Graveyard
hacker_news.summary: The "AI Product Graveyard" highlights the numerous artificial intelligence products that have been discontinued or shut down, reflecting the high failure rate in the rapidly evolving AI market.

hacker_news.title: Telus Uses AI to Alter Call-Agent Accents
hacker_news.summary: Telus is employing artificial intelligence to modify the accents of its call center agents, likely to achieve a standardized or preferred vocal profile for customer interactions.

hacker_news.title: GLM-5V-Turbo: Toward a Native Foundation Model for Multimodal Agents
hacker_news.summary: Researchers introduced GLM-5V-Turbo, a new native foundation model designed to advance the development of sophisticated multimodal agents.

hacker_news.title: Zuckerberg 'personally authorized' Meta's copyright infringement, publishers say
hacker_news.summary: Publishers allege in a lawsuit that Mark Zuckerberg personally approved Meta's use of copyrighted material to train its AI models, constituting copyright infringement.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Airbyte Agents – context for agents across multiple data sources
hacker_news.summary: Airbyte Agents introduces context capabilities for AI agents, allowing them to operate effectively across diverse, multiple data sources.

github_trending.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
github_trending.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

github_trending.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides production-grade engineering skills designed to enhance the capabilities of AI coding agents.

github_trending.description: ⚡ TabPFN: Foundation Model for Tabular Data ⚡
github_trending.summary: TabPFN is a foundation model for tabular data that leverages a large pre-trained transformer to achieve state-of-the-art performance on various classification and regression tasks with minimal training required.

github_trending.description: Open source DocuSign alternative. Create, fill, and sign digital documents ✍️
github_trending.summary: DocuSeal is an open-source alternative to DocuSign, enabling users to create, fill out, and digitally sign electronic documents.

github_trending.description: ~95% on SimpleQA (e.g. Qwen3.6-27B on a 3090). Supports all local and cloud LLMs (llama.cpp, Ollama, Google, ...). 10+ search engines - arXiv, PubMed, your private documents. Everything Local & Encrypted.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a local, encrypted framework for deep research using various LLMs (like Qwen, Llama.cpp, Ollama) and over ten search engines, achieving high accuracy on tasks like SimpleQA.
Ladybird is a truly independent web browser built from scratch, featuring a modern rendering engine and a focus on web standards compliance.

github_trending.description: InsForge is a Postgres-based backend with auth, storage, compute, hosting, and AI gateway. Built for coding agents.
github_trending.summary: InsForge is a Postgres-based backend providing authentication, storage, compute, hosting, and an AI gateway, specifically engineered to support coding agents.

github_trending.description: An autonomous agent for deep financial research
github_trending.summary: Dexter is an autonomous agent designed for deep financial research, leveraging AI to analyze complex market data and generate insights.
The `anthropics/financial-services` repository likely provides tools, code, or examples related to applying Anthropic's AI models to tasks within the financial services industry.
